title: Cheesy Venezuelan Cheese Quesadilla
description: A romantic Venezuelan dish featuring a citrusy beurre-monte sauce, pulled cheese, and crunchy chestnuts. Perfect for a low-carb-friendly dinner or snack.

ingredients.name: Chestnut flour tortillas
ingredients.notes: preferably made with chestnut flour for extra flavor

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a mild, neutral oil for best flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: use a freshly ground black pepper for best flavor

ingredients.name: Chestnuts
ingredients.notes: roasted and chopped for best flavor

instructions: Preheat a griddle or skillet to 325°F (165°C).
Spread 1 tsp of olive oil on a chestnut flour tortilla.
Place 2 oz of Queso fresco on half of the tortilla.
Add 1 oz of shredded Monterey Jack cheese on top of the Queso fresco.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the tortilla is lightly browned and the cheese is melted.
Flip the quesadilla and cook for an additional 2 minutes or until the other side is lightly browned.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling ingredients.
While the quesadillas are cooking, prepare the beurre-monte sauce by whisking together the lime juice, 2 tbsp of softened butter, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Once the quesadillas are cooked, brush the beurre-monte sauce on top of each one.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
Roast 1 cup of chestnuts in a 400°F oven for 10-12 minutes or until tender, then chop and set aside for serving.
